name: SeTcbPrivilege-Sliver-C2-Extension

documentation.readme: Sliver C2 extension for Windows local privilege escalation via SeTcbPrivilege abuse. Enables SeTcbPrivilege, hooks AcquireCredentialsHandleW to spoof the SYSTEM LUID, then creates/starts a Windows service running an arbitrary command as NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM.

arguments.name: -S
arguments.description: CLI flag '-S' accepted by the tool; behavior is implementation-defined and should be confirmed against the upstream README.

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.description: Target port (default: service default).

arguments.name: --interface
arguments.description: Network interface to bind to (e.g. wlan0mon, eth0).

arguments.name: --verbose
arguments.description: Increase log verbosity (repeatable for higher levels).

arguments.name: --threads
arguments.description: Number of concurrent workers.

arguments.name: --timeout
arguments.description: Per-operation timeout in seconds.

arguments.name: --rate
arguments.description: Packet / request rate limit (packets/sec).

arguments.description: HTTP User-Agent header value.

arguments.name: --cookie
arguments.description: HTTP Cookie header value.

arguments.name: --header

arguments.name: --json

arguments.description: Disable ANSI color in output.

arguments.description: Force the action even if a safety check would block it.

arguments.description: BPF filter expression for packet selection.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'post_exploit' phase against surfaces [shell_windows, shell_linux, ad] in the Post-Exploitation category. Fits when the engagement needs: SeTcbPrivilege-Sliver-C2-Extension.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: Sliver C2 extension for Windows local privilege escalation via SeTcbPrivilege abuse. Enables SeTcbPrivilege, hooks AcquireCredentialsHandleW to spoof the SYSTEM LUID, then creates/starts a Windows service running an arbitrary command as NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM.
documentation.how_to_use: Clone or install `SeTcbPrivilege-Sliver-C2-Extension` from its upstream repository, then run `SeTcbPrivilege-Sliver-C2-Extension --help` (or the README entrypoint) to confirm flags.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.

use_cases: Use AFTER the chain has gained a shell on the target. Each post-exploit method is per-step ACCEPT gated.
Use as a candidate follow-up for credential harvesting, lateral movement, or persistence — never inlined into argv.
